_note: Frozen calibration-sim baseline. Re-baselined 2026-06-10 for Daniel's coach-audit R2 (curated REP_RANGES spec → laterals/rear/calves [12,20], curls/triceps [10,15], leg ext/curl [15,20]). The curated table is the rep TRUTH, applied regardless of dp_rep_class_v1 (the flag governs DERIVATION for non-curated names only), so the wider floors move the rMin this sim back-solves the demonstrated working load at → the path-B prescription hash legitimately shifts (OLD 5414275e… → NEW 80d35da1…). VERIFIED BENIGN, learning IMPROVED: convergedPct 2.9→5.1, meanFinalAbsErr 0.259→0.258, machine signed-err -0.119→-0.104; crater===0 HELD, absurd-OVERSHOOT(>3x)===0 HELD, nonAdaptation 2=2, stuckHigh 5=5, stuckLow 85=85, absurd 7=7, barbell/cable/dumbbell bias flat. The ONLY metric that rose is oscillation 24→26 (+2): the wider rep bands let two borderline profiles bounce one extra weight step while converging closer to capacity — a saw-tooth-detector threshold artifact in a cohort that overall converges BETTER, not an instability regression (crater/absurd/stuck all flat, convergence up). dp_load_model_v1 is pinned OFF in resetStore (path-B), so the flip does NOT move this hash. nProfiles=12 (CI tier).
